channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrats to elect new leader as party struggles to repair brand
item.description: Democrats are poised to elect a new leader in a low-profile Democratic National Committee election that could have big implications for the party’s future.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How non-violent undocumented immigrants could be swept up in raids
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In two weeks of the second Trump administration, the White House is staying true to tough immigration policies with ICE raids across the country. Several have happened in the tri-state area.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Air traffic controllers were initially offered buyouts and told to consider leaving government
item.description: Just a day before a deadly midair collision at Reagan National Airport outside Washington, D.C., employees at the Federal Aviation Administration were sent an offer to resign with eight months' pay.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to consider an effort to establish the nation’s first publicly funded religious charter school
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed Friday to hear arguments over a proposal to establish the nation’s first publicly funded religious charter school in Oklahoma.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration to pause federal government websites in effort to eliminate DEI, anti-Trump content
item.description: The Trump administration will put a pause on most federal government websites Friday evening in an effort to eliminate DEI content and any language that is opposed to President Trump’s agenda, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Democrats seek a new leader with Minnesota’s DFL party chair in contention
item.description: MPR News politics editor and host Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the Democratic National Committee’s election of a new party chair and the Minnesotan who’s campaigning for the job. Later, a conversation with newly elected Minnesota Republican Party Chair Alex Plechash.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants to cut the federal workforce. Who they are and what that means
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to cut the federal workforce by 10 percent might not save as much money as hoped and could have unintended consequences. NPR explains why.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Pilots have long worried about DC's complex airspace contributing to a catastrophe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The airspace around Washington, D.C., is congested and complex — a combination aviation experts have long worried could lead to catastrophe.
item.dc:creator: RYAN J. FOLEY, JIM MUSTIAN AND MICHAEL BIESECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Doug Burgum as interior secretary after Trump tasked him to boost drilling
item.description: The Senate confirmed Doug Burgum as interior secretary late Thursday after President Donald Trump tapped the North Dakota billionaire to spearhead the Republican administration's ambitions to boost fossil fuel production. The vote was 79-18.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Why some Minnesota law enforcement agencies will not follow Trump’s mass deportation plan
item.description: Besides laws which prevent local police from enforcing immigration law, some officials say they don’t have the resources to take on additional tasks.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: The chairman of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is launching an investigation into NPR and PBS over their alleged "airing of commercials.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: "We’ll be announcing the tariffs on Canada and Mexico for a number of reasons," Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. on defensive over his vaccine views as a key confirmation vote hangs in the balance
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr. 's bid to be the nation's top health official is uncertain after a key Republican joined Democrats to raise persistent concerns over the nominee's deep skepticism of routine childhood vaccinations that prevent deadly diseases. Sen.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds protest immigration policies at Trump Hotel in Vegas
item.description: Around 200 people gathered outside the Trump Hotel in Vegas to push back against the administration's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Sasha Loftis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests FAA's diversity efforts caused safety issues in crash
item.description: Trump weighed in on a crash between an American Airlines plane and a military helicopter, claiming it “should have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr., Patel hearings set to consume Capitol Hill
item.description: Hearing-mania is set to consume Capitol Hill on Thursday as a trio of President Trump's top allies appear for high-stakes confirmation showdowns with senators that could make or break their chances of getting across the finish line. Tulsi Gabbard, Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trans community fears Trump's actions will upend legal precedent on prison protections
item.description: One of President Trump's first executive orders impacts transgender inmates incarcerated in federal facilities. It's just one in a series of actions signed by Trump that target the trans community.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Center for Victims of Torture dramatically slashes workforce after Trump administration orders
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive orders forced many international humanitarian organizations, like St. Paul-based Center for Victims of Torture, to cut spending and furlough staff.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump appeals hush money criminal conviction
item.description: President Trump appealed his New York hush money criminal conviction and has brought on a new team of attorneys to represent him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Medicaid, SNAP, other benefits safe for Nevadans, Lombardo says
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo issued a statement Tuesday to clarify what is and is not affected by executive orders issued by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from the first Trump White House briefing
item.description: President Trump’s press secretary Karoline Leavitt held her first White House briefing Tuesday, fielding questions from reporters for just more than 45 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Nikki Fried vows to rebuild Florida Democrats as re-elected chair
item.description: Nikki Fried was reelected Saturday as chair of the Florida Democratic Party, which is seeking to regain relevance in a state that has tilted decidedly toward the Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
